mission.title: telepresence: [v2] - Feature request - Develop an ability to intercept several ports of deployment
mission.description: [v2] - Feature request - Develop an ability to intercept several ports of deployment. Requested by 84+ users.

steps.title: Check current telepresence setup
steps.description:
Verify your telepresence version and configuration:
```bash
telepresence version
telepresence status
```
This feature requires telepresence v2 or later.

steps.title: Review the feature request
steps.description: Users need the ability to intercept multiple ports of a single deployment simultaneously. In telepresence v1, only single-port intercepts were supported, which prevented migration to v2 for services exposing multiple ports.

steps.title: Use multi-port intercept
steps.description:
Telepresence v2 added support for intercepting multiple ports. Use the `--port` flag multiple times:
```bash
telepresence intercept <deployment> --port 8080:8080 --port 9090:9090
```
Or check the telepresence documentation for the latest multi-port intercept syntax.

Upgrade telepresence if needed:
```bash
brew upgrade datawire/blackbird/telepresence
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test multi-port intercepts:
```bash
telepresence connect
telepresence intercept <deployment> --port 8080:8080 --port 9090:9090
telepresence status
```
Confirm that traffic on both ports is correctly routed to your local machine.

resolution.summary: Telepresence v2 added the ability to intercept multiple ports of a single deployment simultaneously, enabling migration from v1 for services that expose multiple ports.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with telepresence installed. Install via: brew install datawire/blackbird/telepresence (macOS) or binary download.
